name: pydicom
tagline: Reference skill for reading, writing, anonymizing, and converting medical DICOM imaging files with the pydicom Python library.
verdict: Solid, low-risk library guide: no network egress, no secret access, no hidden or manipulative instructions. One real quality flaw: the anonymization example's comment claims dates get 'a random offset' but the code hardcodes StudyDate to a fixed value, so a user copying it verbatim gets weaker PHI protection than the text implies.

highlights.summary: Installs from PyPI with no version pins (`uv pip install pydicom`, `pylibjpeg`, `python-gdcm`, etc.) — legitimate, well-known packages, but unpinned installs mean the exact code executed is not reprod
No explicit 'do not use when' guidance anywhere in the file; nothing tells the agent when pydicom is the wrong tool (e.g. non-DICOM imaging formats, DICOMweb/network PACS protocols).
Anonymization example is internally inconsistent: comment says '# Shift dates by a random offset' (line 216) but the code directly below hardcodes `ds.StudyDate = '20000101'` (line 217) — not random,
